title: Fix Admin panel stuck on 'Verifying admin access' — page never loads

requirements.text: Fix the Admin page so it no longer gets stuck indefinitely on the 'Verifying admin access' loading state. After a user authenticates via Internet Identity, the admin access check must always settle (resolve or reject) and either render the admin dashboard or display an unauthorized message — the page must never hang in a loading state.

quotes: Verifying admin access is showing but nothing is opening, the page is not loading

acceptanceCriteria: After logging in with an admin account, the Admin page fully loads and displays the admin dashboard without remaining stuck on 'Verifying admin access'.
The admin access check query/logic always transitions out of its loading state — either to a success state (admin content rendered) or an error/unauthorized state (access denied message shown).
If the user is not an admin, a clear 'Access Denied' or equivalent message is displayed instead of an infinite spinner.
The fix works consistently on repeated logins without requiring a page refresh.
No regression: non-admin routes continue to function normally.

requirements.text: Audit and fix the admin-gating query logic in useQueries.ts to ensure the admin role check never enters a permanently pending state. The query must handle all edge cases (actor not ready, identity loading, backend returning no result) by providing explicit fallback values or enabled conditions so the query always settles.

quotes: Verifying admin access is showing but nothing is opening, the page is not loading

acceptanceCriteria: The admin role check query in useQueries.ts has an `enabled` guard that prevents it from running until the actor and identity are fully initialized.
The query has a defined `staleTime` or `retry` policy that prevents it from indefinitely retrying on failure.
The query always resolves to a boolean (true/false) or throws a catchable error — it never stays in `isLoading: true` indefinitely.
Admin.tsx reads the settled query result and renders the appropriate UI state (loading → content or unauthorized) within a reasonable timeout.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All fixes must remain within the single-actor backend architecture; no new backend endpoints unless strictly necessary.
nonGoals: Changing the authentication provider or switching away from Internet Identity.
Redesigning the Admin UI layout or adding new admin features.
Modifying backend role/access control logic unless the frontend fix alone is insufficient.
